description:
src/lib.rs:46: 'pub struct CachingStore<S: SecretStore>' has no Debug impl. Common Rust API guideline (C-COMMON-TRAITS in the API guidelines) is that public types should derive or implement Debug.

Cannot derive directly because:
- Arc<S> requires S: Debug — fine if we add S: Debug bound on the impl, but bounds on impls don't propagate well to dyn.
- tokio::sync::Mutex<T> implements Debug for any T (via 'no contents' format).

A manual impl that does not print bytes is appropriate (cached secret bytes must not be printed):
    impl<S: SecretStore> std::fmt::Debug for CachingStore<S> {
        fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
            f.debug_struct("CachingStore")
                .field("ttl", &self.ttl)
                .field("cache", &"<redacted>")
                .finish()
        }
    }

P4 (opinion / hygiene). Skip if the maintainer's style elsewhere is to omit Debug on small wrapper types — verify against secretx-core conventions before applying.
title: No Debug impl on CachingStore — derives or manual impl missing
